tasks.title: Implement ticket assignment endpoint
tasks.description: Add PATCH /api/tickets/:id/assignee handler that validates the request payload, updates the ticket in the store, and returns the updated ticket or appropriate error codes.
tasks.design_notes: Use existing Zod validation utilities, return HTTP 200 with the updated ticket JSON to keep response shape consistent across APIs.
tasks.advice: Implement the PATCH route, use Zod for payload validation, call MemoryTicketStore.assignTicket, and return the updated ticket with status 200. Add proper error handling for missing ticket (404) and validation errors (400).

tasks.title: Add assignment logic to MemoryTicketStore
tasks.description: Extend MemoryTicketStore to support updating a ticket's assignee field while preserving in‑memory state and emitting events for consistency.
tasks.design_notes: Introduce a new assignTicket(id, assignee) method that conforms to the TicketStore interface; ensure existing get/set methods remain unchanged.
tasks.advice: Add an assignTicket method to MemoryTicketStore that updates the assignee field in the in‑memory map and emits any required events. Update the TicketStore interface accordingly.

tasks.title: Create assignment UI controls in React
tasks.description: Build a dropdown of agents and an Assign button in the ticket detail view, calling the shared API client to PATCH the assignee.
tasks.design_notes: Leverage shared/api.ts for type‑safe calls; update local component state on success to avoid a full page reload; reuse existing TicketDetail component styling.
tasks.advice: Create UI controls (dropdown + button) in the ticket detail component, call the shared API client to PATCH /api/tickets/:id/assignee, and optimistically update local state on success.

tasks.title: Write tests for assignment flow
tasks.description: Add Vitest/Supertest cases covering successful assignment, validation failures, and unauthorized access scenarios.
tasks.design_notes: Mock MemoryTicketStore where appropriate; keep tests fast and deterministic; assert response shape matches other endpoints.
tasks.advice: Write Supertest integration tests for the assignment endpoint covering success, validation failures, and unauthorized access. Mock MemoryTicketStore to keep tests deterministic.

tasks.title: Build SQLiteTicketStore with migrations
tasks.description: Create a SQLite‑backed TicketStore implementation, schema migrations, and seed logic mirroring the in‑memory data shape.
tasks.design_notes: Use better-sqlite3 for synchronous queries; place migration scripts under db/migrations; ensure the store implements the same TicketStore interface for seamless swapping.
tasks.advice: Start the SQLiteTicketStore implementation, define the schema, and add migration scripts under db/migrations.

tasks.title: Wire server to use configurable TicketStore
tasks.description: Introduce an environment variable to select between MemoryTicketStore and SQLiteTicketStore at startup and inject the chosen store into the Express app.
tasks.design_notes: Apply the factory pattern in server/app.ts; keep route definitions agnostic of the underlying store implementation.
tasks.advice: Introduce an env variable (e.g., TICKET_STORE) and a factory in server/app.ts to instantiate the selected store.

tasks.title: Implement admin auth middleware and protect audit routes
tasks.description: Complete the placeholder auth middleware to verify JWT admin claims and reject non‑admin requests with 403 on audit‑related endpoints.
tasks.design_notes: Reuse existing auth utilities; add role checking logic; register middleware only on /api/audit routes to keep other endpoints unaffected.
tasks.advice: Complete auth middleware: verify JWT, ensure admin claim, return 403 for non‑admin on /api/audit routes.

tasks.title: Add audit log endpoint and frontend panel
tasks.description: Create GET /api/audit that returns recent audit entries from SQLite and display them in a new admin‑only panel in the React dashboard.
tasks.design_notes: Persist audit records via a dedicated SQLiteAuditLog table; conditionally render the panel based on user.role === 'admin'; use shared/api.ts for fetching logs.
tasks.advice: After SQLiteTicketStore is ready, add GET /api/audit endpoint and a corresponding admin‑only React panel.

tasks.title: Implement saved views UI and persistence
tasks.description: Add a panel allowing agents to save custom ticket filter configurations and persist them using the SQLiteTicketStore.
tasks.design_notes: Create a SavedViews component with create/read/delete actions; store view definitions in a SQLiteSavedViews table; expose REST endpoints (GET/POST/DELETE) following existing API patterns.
